name: Makaha
desc: End-of-the-road beach valley — legendary surf break, affordable beachfront, gorgeous valley, isolated and rough edges.
insider: Makaha Beach has world-class surf in winter with waves regularly hitting 15-25 feet — the Buffalo Big Board Surfing Classic held here since 1977 is a legendary cultural event
In summer the bay goes glass-calm and becomes one of the best snorkeling spots on the west side, with spinner dolphins frequently swimming into the bay in early morning
The valley hike to Kaneaki Heiau, a restored 15th-century Hawaiian temple maintained by the Bishop Museum, is one of the most important cultural sites on Oahu
Makaha Valley Country Club is one of the most affordable golf courses on the island with jaw-dropping Waianae Range backdrops on every hole
Yokohama Bay (Keawaula) at the end of the road past Makaha is the most remote accessible beach on Oahu — wild, empty, and hauntingly beautiful
This is one of the only places on Oahu where you can experience genuine silence — no planes overhead, no traffic hum, no construction noise
Car theft and break-ins are a serious problem — locals advise driving an older vehicle with nothing inside worth stealing and leaving doors unlocked to prevent window smashing
The surf community here is deeply local and territorial — be respectful in the lineup, don't drop in on locals, and you'll be welcomed
Makaha Shores and Makaha Surfside condos offer some of the cheapest oceanfront living in all of Hawaii — units occasionally go for under $200K
The Hawaiian community here is strong and proud — participate in beach cleanups and community events if you move here, it's how you earn respect
